node: dgx3 (internal 8-GPU sm_80 datacenter server; hostname redacted)
goal: GLM-5.2 running on dgx3 performant, using the full GPUs and RAM

what_changed.from: tools/glm52_stage_serve_dgx3.sh pins ALL MoE experts to host RAM (--n-cpu-moe 999); only GPU0 does work; decode is host-expert-GEMM-bound (the #971 wall)
what_changed.to: full model resident across GPUs 1-7 with experts ON GPU HBM (-ngl 999, NO --n-cpu-moe); the whole 434 GiB Q4_K_M checkpoint lives in VRAM
what_changed.one_line: moved the expert GEMM off the host onto 7 GPUs — exactly the #971 mandate

serve.model_quant: unsloth/GLM-5.2-GGUF UD-Q4_K_M (11 shards, ~434 GiB / 753.86 B params)
serve.key_flag: NO --n-cpu-moe => experts stay on GPU; default split-mode=layer spreads layers across the 7 visible GPUs
serve.devices: GPUs 1-7 (GPU0 left to the peer fak serves on :8000/:8001 — not touched)

vram_resident_mib.note: the ~433 GiB sum matches the on-disk model size: the entire Q4_K_M checkpoint (experts included) is resident in GPU HBM across the 7 GPUs

throughput_witness.method: llama.cpp native /completion endpoint timings (predicted_per_second = decode, prompt_per_second = prefill); two stable runs
throughput_witness.decode_per_gpu_util_pct: 12-16 (layer-split pipeline: each GPU active ~1/7 of the time)
throughput_witness.label: OBSERVED (third-party engine, llama.cpp) — not reported as fak's own native throughput

fak_fronting.note: fak's gateway fronts the multi-GPU endpoint and accounts tokens + cache.
fak_fronting.honest_caveat: this 'dev' fak build leaves message.content empty in the NON-streaming serialization; the real generation is witnessed by completion_tokens=60 + finish_reason=length + the ~2.9 s wall (a mock would be instant) and by the direct :8002 smoke/throughput witness above

honesty_boundary.engine: llama.cpp serves the weights; fak fronts/governs them. This is external-engine serving evidence, kept separate from fak's NATIVE in-kernel glm_moe_dsa evidence (experiments/glm-gpu-witness/a100-glm52-59bc40f.json).
honesty_boundary.all_8_gpus: 7/8 GPUs used by design — GPU0 carries the peer fak serves on a shared box. A dedicated box can use all 8 via DEVICES=0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7 (the model fits in any 7, so 8 adds KV-cache headroom, not capacity).
honesty_boundary.headroom: single-stream layer-split leaves per-GPU util at ~14%; batched concurrency or --split-mode row could raise aggregate throughput further (not needed to clear 'performant').
